title: Multiply fractions and whole numbers on the number line
description: Practice multiplying fractions and whole numbers using a number line.
topic_path: Multiplying fractions and whole numbers visually
Multiply fractions and whole numbers on the number line

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which expressions match the following number line?**

[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Starting from $0$, the arrow moves a length of $\dfrac12$ on the number line $\greenD5$ times to get to the point.


[[☃ image 1]]
We can express this movement by adding $\dfrac12+\dfrac12+\dfrac12+\dfrac12+\dfrac12$ .
We can also express this movement by multiplying $\greenD5 \times \dfrac12$  .

The following expressions match the number line:

* $\dfrac12+\dfrac12+\dfrac12+\dfrac12+\dfrac12$

* $5 \times \dfrac12$

options.alt: A number line labeled zero-halves to six-halves with labeled tick marks at every one-half unit. A green arrow jumps left to right from zero-halves to one-half, from one-half to two-halves, from two-halves to three-halves, from three-halves to four-halves, and then from four-halves to five-halves.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which expressions match the following number line?**



[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Starting from $0$, the arrow moves a length of $\dfrac12$ on the number line $\greenD3$ times to get to the point.


[[☃ image 1]]
We can express this movement by adding $\dfrac{1}{2}+\dfrac{1}{2} + \dfrac{1}{2}$ .
We can also express this movement by multiplying $\greenD3 \times \dfrac12$  .

The following expressions match the number line:

* $\dfrac{1}{2}+\dfrac{1}{2} + \dfrac{1}{2}$

* $3 \times \dfrac12$

options.alt: A number line labeled zero-halves to six-halves with labeled tick marks at every one-half unit. A green arrow jumps left to right from zero-halves to one-half, from one-half to two-halves, and then from two-halves to three-halves.

**Which expressions match the following number line?**


[[☃ image 1]]

hints:
Starting from $0$, the arrow moves a length of $\dfrac{1}{12}$ on the number line $\greenD5$ times to get to the point.



[[☃ image 1]]
We can express this movement by adding $\dfrac{1}{12}+\dfrac{1}{12} + \dfrac{1}{12} + \dfrac{1}{12}+ \dfrac{1}{12}$ .
We can also express this movement by multiplying $\greenD5 \times \dfrac{1}{12}$  .

The following expressions match the number line:

* $\dfrac{1}{12}+\dfrac{1}{12} + \dfrac{1}{12} + \dfrac{1}{12}+ \dfrac{1}{12}$

* $5 \times \dfrac{1}{12}$

A green arrow jumps left to right from zero-twelfths to one-twelfth, from one-twelfth to two-twelfths, from two-twelfths to three-twelfths, from three-twelfths to four-twelfths, and then from four-twelfths to five-twelfths.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which expressions match the following number line?**


[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Starting from $0$, the arrow moves a length of $\dfrac32$ on the number line $\greenD2$ times to get to the point.


[[☃ image 1]]
We can express this movement by adding $\dfrac{3}{2}+\dfrac{3}{2}$  .
We can also express this movement by multiplying $\greenD2 \times\dfrac{3}{2}$  .

The following expressions match the number line:

* $\dfrac{3}{2}+\dfrac{3}{2}$ 

* $2 \times \dfrac32$

options.alt: A number line labeled zero-halves to eight-halves with labeled tick marks at every one-half unit. A green arrow jumps right from zero-halves to three-halves and then from three-halves to six-halves.
